title: rubus koehleri subsp. spinulifer
intro: Rubus koehleri subsp. spinulifer, also known as the Korean black raspberry or Korean black thorn, is a subspecies of the Rubus koehleri plant, a type of bramble native to East Asia. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rubus koehleri subsp. spinulifer include its potential to protect against cancer, cardiovascular disease, and neurodegenerative disorders, as well as its ability to improve cognitive function and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, are commonly used in traditional medicine in Korea and other parts of Asia to promote overall health and well-being.
